name: Assist Mode
description: A named accessibility preset or mode that enables one or more gameplay assists such as timing forgiveness, input help, reduced penalties, or navigation support.
design_notes: Assist mode should be explicit about which features it enables, who owns the profile setting, and where it is restricted. Define assist_mode_id, intensity tiers, timing and input multipliers, privacy or telemetry boundaries, and whether the setting is per-save, per-account, or per-local-player.
design_purpose: Bundle related support options into a discoverable path for players who need help quickly.
Let players adjust specific barriers such as timing, aiming, navigation, or penalty severity.
Provide support without implying that players are using the game incorrectly.
player_fantasy: The player feels welcomed by a game that can meet them where they are without shaming the choice.

parameters.name: assist_mode_id
parameters.description: Preset or custom mode selected by the player.

parameters.name: assist_feature_ids
parameters.description: Feature toggles included in the selected assist mode.

parameters.name: assist_intensity_level
parameters.description: Preset strength used when applying bundled assists.

parameters.name: timing_assist_multiplier
parameters.description: Multiplier applied to timing windows such as jumps, parries, or prompts.

parameters.name: input_assist_policy_id
parameters.description: Input helper policy applied before gameplay systems consume actions.

parameters.name: profile_visibility_policy_id
parameters.description: Disclosure rule for saves, analytics, or ranked modes.

edge_cases: Assist mode is enabled after a checkpoint but before a deterministic challenge result is submitted.
Two local players use different assist modes in the same split-screen or couch co-op session.
A save created with assist features is loaded on a platform where one input helper is unavailable.
Telemetry or achievements record assist usage without clear privacy and disclosure rules.
common_bugs: Assist mode changes UI labels but does not update the feature registry consumed by gameplay systems.
Timing multipliers stack with coyote time and input buffering multiple times after scene reload.
One local player toggles assist mode and changes every player profile in the session.
Challenge restrictions disable assists silently instead of explaining the locked policy.
balancing_notes: Assist mode should target barriers rather than broadly flattening every system unless the preset clearly says so.
Leaderboard and ranked restrictions should be handled through transparent mode policy, not hidden penalties.
Custom assist menus reduce frustration when players need one support feature but not a full preset.
accessibility_notes: Place assist mode near first-launch setup and keep it reachable from pause menus.
Avoid moralized labels; describe practical effects such as wider timing windows or reduced damage.

implementation_notes.unity: Use an assist_feature_registry keyed by assist_mode_id and profile id. Gameplay systems request resolved assists such as timing_assist_multiplier or input_assist_policy_id from one service, while UI Toolkit offers live preview and reset-to-default flows.
implementation_notes.godot: Store assist settings in a Resource-backed profile and expose a signal when committed settings change. Movement, combat, and UI scripts should subscribe to resolved feature ids rather than reading menu controls directly.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a player settings subsystem plus Data Assets for assist presets. Gameplay Ability, Enhanced Input, and UI systems should query the same replicated or local profile, with server authority enforcing ranked restrictions.
implementation_notes.web: Keep assist profile data versioned in storage and resolve feature ids before the simulation tick. For local multiplayer, key settings by local_player_id so one player can use assists without changing another player.

example_games.title: Celeste
example_games.note: Uses an assist mode to expose practical support options for movement challenges.

example_games.title: Forza Motorsport
example_games.note: Uses driving assists that can be adjusted to player needs and skill.
